description: Synthetic test fixtures for KB injection testing — RAG retrieval accuracy, numerical claim extraction, domain-specific retrieval, and hallucination detection.

quantum_computing_overview.md.purpose: Test RAG retrieval accuracy and hallucination verification for scientific/technical content

key_facts.claim: A qubit state is described as alpha|0> + beta|1> where |alpha|^2 + |beta|^2 = 1
Hadamard gate creates equal superposition: H|0> = (|0> + |1>)/sqrt(2)
Shor's algorithm factors large integers in polynomial time O((log N)^2 * (log log N) * (log log log N))
Shor's algorithm was developed by Peter Shor in 1994
Grover's algorithm finds a target in O(sqrt(N)) evaluations, published by Lov Grover in 1996
BQP is the class of decision problems solvable by a quantum computer in polynomial time with error probability at most 1/3
QMA is the quantum analogue of NP
CNOT gate combined with single-qubit rotations forms a universal gate set
Bell state: |Phi+> = (|00> + |11>)/sqrt(2)

financial_report_q3_2025.md.purpose: Test numerical claim extraction and verification for financial data

key_facts.claim: Meridian Technologies Q3 2025 total revenue was $847.3M
Revenue increased 12.4% year-over-year
Net income was $127.6M
Total employees: 4,200
Headquartered in Austin, Texas
Diluted EPS was $2.84
Cloud Platform Services revenue was $512.8M (60.5% of total)
Annual Recurring Revenue was $3.24B
Net Revenue Retention Rate was 118%
Free Cash Flow was $198.2M

api_documentation.md.purpose: Test code/technical domain retrieval for API documentation

key_facts.claim: API base URL is https://api.datastream.io/api/v2/
GET /api/v2/streams lists all streams
POST /api/v2/ingest publishes events to a stream, max 1000 events per request
POST /api/v2/query executes analytical queries using DSQL
Professional tier rate limit is 1,000 requests per minute
Authentication requires both Bearer token and API key (X-API-Key header)
Bearer tokens expire after 3600 seconds (1 hour)
Maximum ingest request body size is 5 MB
Supported output formats: json, csv, parquet, arrow
Version v1.x is deprecated and will be sunset on March 1, 2026
Each organization can have up to 25 active API keys
Webhook payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256

medical_research_summary.md.purpose: Test domain-specific retrieval and numerical verification for clinical/medical content

key_facts.claim: CLARITY-7 was a Phase III clinical trial
The drug studied is Nexoril (compound NXR-4821)
Total enrollment was 1,247 participants randomized 1:1
Responder rate: 62% Nexoril vs 34% placebo
Statistical significance: p < 0.001
Trial completed March 2025
Conducted across 43 clinical sites in US, UK, Germany, and Australia
Treatment duration was 24 weeks with 12-week follow-up
Nexoril is a selective modulator of the JAK1/TYK2 signaling pathway
NDA submitted to FDA August 2025, PDUFA date April 2026
Number needed to treat (NNT) was 3.6

project_management_notes.md.purpose: Test personal/project domain retrieval for internal organizational content

Backend migration is 78% complete

mixed_claims_document.md.purpose: Test hallucination detection — document contains 10 specific claims, 7 correct and 3 intentionally wrong

key_facts.claim: Python was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991
JavaScript was created by Brendan Eich in 1995 at Netscape
HTTP/2 was standardized in 2012 as RFC 7540
key_facts.actual_fact: HTTP/2 was standardized in May 2015 (RFC 7540). The year 2012 is wrong.

key_facts.claim: TCP/IP was formally adopted by ARPANET on January 1, 1983
TLS 1.3 was published in August 2018 as RFC 8446
Hubble Space Telescope was launched on April 24, 1990 aboard Discovery
JWST primary mirror is 8.2 meters in diameter
key_facts.actual_fact: JWST's primary mirror is 6.5 meters in diameter, not 8.2 meters.

key_facts.claim: Alan Turing published 'On Computable Numbers' in 1936
FFT was published by Cooley and Tukey in 1965
Human Genome Project was declared complete in June 2000
key_facts.actual_fact: A draft sequence was announced in June 2000, but the project was declared complete in April 2003.
